 {"id":"ft-ombfl.13","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness] CI wiring: dedicated GPU-test job + diff artifact upload + perf-report artifact","description":"# GPU harness — CI wiring

## Background
The harness binary (GPU_SCAFFOLD), e2e wrapper (GPU_E2E),
fixtures (multiple beads), and frame-time reporter (GPU_FRAMETIME)
all need to run in CI. This bead is the integration into GitHub
Actions.

## Why this matters
- Tests that don't run in CI don't catch regressions.
- Diff artifacts uploaded on failure make triage immediate.
- Perf reports archived per CI run enable historical trend analysis.

## Scope
1. Add a dedicated CI job (or extend existing ci.yml):
   - Job name: `gpu-regression`.
   - Runner: macOS (per design doc — pinned for determinism).
   - Steps: checkout + cache cargo + run scripts/test-gpu-harness.sh.
   - On failure: upload /tmp/gpu-harness-<ts>/diffs/ and
     /tmp/gpu-harness-<ts>/summary.html as artifacts.
   - Always: upload tests/golden/gpu/perf-report.json as artifact.
2. Pin the macOS runner version (e.g., macos-15) so renderer
   determinism doesn't drift.
3. Add a "release-blocker" status: if gpu-regression fails on a
   release tag, block the release workflow.
4. Document the renderer-determinism caveat in the workflow
   YAML and in tests/golden/gpu/README.md.

## Out of scope
- Linux GPU CI (renderer determinism on llvmpipe is a separate
  effort).
- Self-hosted GPU runners (premature optimization).

## Approach
1. Read existing .github/workflows/ to find the right insertion
   point (extend ci.yml or add a new file).
2. Add the gpu-regression job.
3. Wire artifact upload via actions/upload-artifact@v4 (if-no-files-
   found=ignore for the perf report; warn for diffs since absence
   means all passed).
4. Test by inducing a regression PR; confirm CI fails and artifacts
   are uploaded.

## Acceptance criteria
- gpu-regression job exists in CI; runs on every PR + main push.
- Failure uploads diffs + summary.html.
- Always uploads perf-report.json.
- Job runs in <10 min on the pinned macOS runner.
- Release workflow respects gpu-regression status.

## Testing
- CI dry-run: open a PR with a one-pixel mutation in a fixture;
  confirm CI fails, artifacts uploaded.
- Performance: assert wall time <10 min.
- Logging: CI log contains the JSON-line events from
  scripts/test-gpu-harness.sh.

## Risks / considerations
- macOS runners are slower and more expensive than Linux. Make
  sure cargo caching is aggressive.
- Pinning macos-15 means we'll need to update annually when GitHub
  retires that image.
- If goldens drift on a runner image update, treat that as a
  separate ticket — don't accept goldens silently.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- ci

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD, GPU_E2E, GPU_FRAMETIME (prerequisites).
- .github/workflows/ (existing CI infra).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass 2026-04-27 (CI specifics)

### Runtime budget
Target <5 min wall (was <10). With caching:
- Restore cargo cache: <30s.
- Build harness binary (incremental): <2 min.
- Run harness (≤30 fixtures × 50ms): <5s.
- Upload artifacts: <30s.
- Total: ~3 min.
If first-build (no cache), bump to <8 min.

### Job name and YAML location
- Workflow file: extend existing `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (don't
  proliferate workflow files unless needed).
- Job name: `gpu-regression`.
- Required-status: yes (block PRs that fail this).

### macOS runner deprecation handling
- Pin `runs-on: macos-15` initially.
- Document a "runner-image-update" follow-on bead due in ~12 months
  (when macos-15 retires).
- The runner-update follow-on must regenerate goldens (font/driver
  drift) — file as `[ft-ombfl][maintenance]` follow-on with explicit
  steps.

### perf-baseline file management
- Initial baseline: committed by GPU_FRAMETIME bead.
- Updates: only via `--update-baseline` flag with explicit reviewer
  approval.
- CI does NOT update baseline automatically.

### Caching strategy
- `actions/cache@v4` keyed on Cargo.lock + harness source hash.
- tests/fixtures/fonts/ as a separate static asset (rarely changes;
  cache aggressively).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:30.167334Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T00:16:41.024412Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.13","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:30.167334Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.13","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:11.045486Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.14","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness][stretch] Stress fixtures: many panes / rapid resize / dpi changes / extreme content","description":"# GPU harness — stress fixtures (stretch)

## Background
Once the core fixtures + comparator + CI are landed, this bead adds
stress-class fixtures that exercise the renderer at scale: many
panes (50+), rapid synthetic resize sequences, DPI scaling
transitions, extreme content (1MB scrollback).

## Why this matters
- Real swarm sessions run 6-15 panes routinely; stress at 50+ surfaces
  a different class of bug (texture cache eviction, glyph atlas
  reflow, font cache pressure).
- DPI transitions (drag window between Retina + non-Retina monitors)
  are a known regression class.
- Extreme content (huge scrollback, very long lines) can crash naive
  renderers.

## Scope
Author 4 stress fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/stress/:
1. **stress-50-panes/**: 50-pane grid layout, deterministic content
   per pane.
2. **stress-rapid-resize/**: a sequence of viewport sizes (smoke-
   test the renderer doesn't crash on rapid changes; pin a final-
   state golden after the sequence).
3. **stress-dpi-1.0-2.0/**: same content rendered at dpi=1.0 and
   dpi=2.0; verify no glyph regression at scale.
4. **stress-1mb-scrollback/**: a pane with 1MB of generated content;
   render the bottom-of-scrollback view.

## Out of scope
- Real-time animation testing (timing-sensitive).
- Multi-monitor span fixtures.

## Approach
1. Each fixture's input.json may need a generator script (especially
   stress-50-panes and stress-1mb-scrollback). Use a one-off Python
   or Rust generator stored under tests/golden/gpu/stress/_generators/.
2. Generate goldens via --update-goldens.
3. Verify each runs in <5s wall time (so stress doesn't slow the
   main suite).
4. Decide whether stress runs on every PR or only on release-tag
   builds — recommend release-only for cost.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 4 stress fixtures committed.
- Each runs to PASS.
- stress-50-panes render <5s.
- DPI transition fixture detects glyph regressions at 2x scale.

## Testing
- Each fixture self-tests.
- Stress-50-panes: capture per-fixture render_ms; assert
  proportional to baseline 5-pane fixture (sanity).
- Mutation: introduce a glyph cache regression (force eviction
  every frame); confirm stress-50-panes detects it via render_ms
  spike or visual diff.

## Risks / considerations
- Generator scripts must be deterministic (seed any RNG).
- Stress fixtures should NOT block the main CI lane — run them on
  a separate label-gated workflow or on release tags only.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- stretch

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD, GPU_RENDERER, GPU_CI (prerequisites).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass round 2 (stress-class regression detection)

### Stress regressions are different from fixture failures
A stress fixture might pass the SSIM check but fail because:
- It rendered in 5x baseline time (perf regression).
- It used 10x baseline atlas pages (resource regression).
- It allocated 50x baseline heap (memory regression).

The stress-fixture run must compare against a stress-specific
baseline (NOT the regular fixture-run baseline).

### Stress baseline file
Add tests/golden/gpu/stress/perf-baseline.json with:
- Per-stress-fixture render_ms (median of 3 runs).
- Per-stress-fixture atlas_pages, glyphs_cached.
- Per-stress-fixture max RSS during render (via /usr/bin/time
  or platform equivalent).

Stress regression thresholds:
- render_ms: 30% (stress is more variable).
- atlas_pages: 20%.
- max_rss: 25%.

### Memory leak detection via repeat-render
For stress-rapid-resize: the harness should run the resize
sequence 10x in a single process. If max_rss grows monotonically
across iterations, that's a leak. Flag if growth >10% from first
to tenth iteration.

This is a substantial extra deliverable — moves stress from "P3
stretch" to "P3 with concrete value". OK either way; if you
want to keep stress lean, file the leak-detection as a separate
follow-on under stress.

### Run schedule
- Stress fixtures: run on RELEASE TAG builds only (not every PR)
  to avoid CI cost.
- On main-branch pushes: run stress weekly via cron-scheduled
  workflow.
- On release tags: run stress as a release-blocker.

### Logging
Stress runs emit detailed per-iteration logs to make leak
detection feasible. Log volume larger here:
~10 iterations × 4 fixtures × ~10 events = 400 events per
stress run. Cap at 5000 lines / 5MB; archive via CI artifacts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:30.660582Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T01:12:19.030084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27","stretch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.14","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:08:30.660582Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.14","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:11.462117Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.15","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness] Fixture authoring guide: how to add a new visual regression fixture","description":"# GPU regression harness — fixture authoring guide

## Background
After GPU_SCAFFOLD lands and the first batch of fixtures (text,
CJK, box, cursor, selection, multi-pane) is in place, future
agents will want to add new fixtures (for new bug classes, new
features, new edge cases). Without a guide, each new fixture
becomes an exercise in spelunking through existing fixtures.

## Why this matters
- Onboarding cost: a new agent should be able to author a fixture
  in <30 min from reading the guide alone.
- Consistency: pinned font set, meta.json schema, golden-update
  procedure must stay uniform across all fixtures.
- Coverage: the guide should encourage filing a fixture every
  time a regression slips through (catch bug → file fixture →
  prevent recurrence).

## Scope (single docs file)
docs/gpu-fixture-authoring.md with sections:
1. **Anatomy of a fixture**:
   - input.json schema (required fields, optional fields, examples).
   - meta.json schema (viewport, dpi, color_space, tolerance,
     font_set, blink_disabled, cursor_blink_disabled,
     ime_disabled, etc.).
   - expected.json (optional sanity-check schema).
   - golden.png format expectations (sRGB, RGBA8, dimensions).
2. **Authoring procedure** (step-by-step):
   - Decide what regression class the fixture catches.
   - Pick a fixture name (kebab-case, descriptive, ≤40 chars).
   - Author input.json + meta.json by hand.
   - Run `cargo test --test gpu_regression --features gpu-headless
     -- --update-goldens <fixture-name>` to generate golden.png.
   - Manually inspect golden.png in an image viewer.
   - Run the test without --update-goldens to verify it passes.
   - Commit fixture directory + reference the bug class in commit.
3. **Determinism checklist** (mandatory pre-commit gates):
   - Font set pinned (subset of tests/fixtures/fonts/).
   - cursor_blink_disabled=true.
   - ime_disabled=true.
   - LC_ALL=C, TZ=UTC enforced.
   - No system-fonts fallback.
4. **Common mistakes**:
   - Trailing whitespace in input.json strings (renders differently
     than expected).
   - Mixing sRGB and linear color spaces.
   - Forgetting to disable cursor blink.
   - Using non-OFL fonts.
   - Path with spaces or non-ASCII chars (rejected by loader).
5. **Updating an existing fixture** (when behavior intentionally
   changes):
   - Document why in PR body.
   - Use --update-goldens with manual review.
   - Diff inspect old vs new golden side-by-side.
6. **Adding a fixture for a real regression**:
   - Workflow: bug filed → reproduce locally → create fixture
     that captures the BROKEN state in a "broken-golden.png" →
     fix the bug → re-generate golden against fixed state →
     commit fixture + fix together.

## Out of scope
- Test runner internals (covered by GPU_SCAFFOLD docs).
- CI configuration (covered by GPU_CI).
- Renderer internals (covered by GPU_RENDERER).

## Approach
1. After GPU_SCAFFOLD, GPU_RENDERER, and at least 2 fixture beads
   land, draft this guide using those as concrete reference cases.
2. Test the guide by having a fresh agent author one new fixture
   (e.g., "tab-character handling") using only the guide.
3. Iterate based on the friction points.
4. Cross-link from tests/golden/gpu/README.md and from
   docs/operator-runbook.md.

## Acceptance criteria
- docs/gpu-fixture-authoring.md exists.
- All 6 sections present.
- A fresh agent can author a NEW fixture (not in the original 14
  beads) using only the guide. Success criteria: the new fixture
  passes CI on first attempt.
- Cross-links from tests/golden/gpu/README.md.

## Testing
- Validation walkthrough: have a fresh agent author one new
  fixture from the guide alone. Document any friction in this
  bead's notes.
- Render check: open the markdown in a previewer; section
  hierarchy is clean.
- Cross-link integrity: each "see X" resolves.
- Logging: validation walkthrough emits a JSON-line trace per
  guide step the new agent follows; document any step that
  needed clarification.

## Risks / considerations
- This guide should be SHORT (≤8 KB). Longer = readers skip.
- Keep examples concrete: include a complete worked example of
  authoring a fixture from scratch (input.json + meta.json
  shown in full).
- Don't duplicate content from GPU_SCAFFOLD's README — link.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- docs

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD (the harness this guide explains).
- GPU_RENDERER (the renderer behind the harness).
- ft-ombfl (parent).
- All fixture beads (concrete examples).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:13.794398Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:19.200328Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.15","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:13.794398Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.15","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:17.793471Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.15","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:18.204849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.15","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:18.612667Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.15","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:19.199356Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-ombfl.16","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness][stretch] Explore Linux GPU CI: llvmpipe/swrast determinism + cost analysis vs macos-15","description":"# Linux GPU CI exploration

## Background
GPU_CI (ft-ombfl.13) pins macOS-15 as the GPU regression test
runner. macOS runners are ~5x more expensive than Ubuntu runners
on GitHub Actions ($0.08/min vs $0.016/min as of 2026-Q1).
For a project that runs CI on every PR, the cost adds up.

This bead explores running GPU regression tests on Linux via
llvmpipe (Mesa software rasterizer) or swrast. The renderer-
determinism question is non-trivial: software rasterization
may produce slightly different output from Metal due to
floating-point rounding, AA strategy, etc.

## Why this matters
- CI cost reduction (5x cheaper compute).
- Linux runners are faster to provision (less queue time).
- A Linux-portable harness is more portable in general.

## Scope (P4 stretch — do NOT block other work)
1. **Determinism investigation**: render the same fixtures on
   macOS-Metal vs Linux-llvmpipe. Measure SSIM / L∞ across
   platforms. Decide:
   - Tight enough → run on Linux primarily.
   - Loose → run separately on each, with platform-specific
     goldens.
   - Hopelessly different → keep macOS-only and accept cost.
2. **Cost-benefit analysis**: model the per-month CI cost
   for the project's PR volume; project savings.
3. **Determinism work** (if go-decision):
   - Pin Mesa + llvmpipe versions in CI runner image.
   - Pin LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 and similar env vars.
   - Possibly: use Vulkan + lavapipe instead of OpenGL +
     llvmpipe (more deterministic).
4. **Recommendation**: file a decision bead with the result.

## Out of scope
- Hardware-GPU CI runners (premature optimization).
- Cross-vendor renderer determinism (Intel vs AMD vs NVIDIA
  drivers — out of scope for software-only path).

## Approach
1. Build the harness with a feature flag for software-renderer
   path on Linux.
2. Render the existing fixture set on a Linux dev box; compare
   SSIM / L∞ to macOS goldens.
3. Document the determinism delta in a spike doc:
   docs/spike/gpu-ci-linux-feasibility.md.
4. Cost-benefit table.
5. Go/no-go recommendation.

## Acceptance criteria
- Determinism spike doc exists with concrete SSIM/L∞ numbers
  for ≥5 representative fixtures.
- Cost-benefit table exists with ($/month) projections.
- Go/no-go recommendation lands as a follow-on bead (one of:
  (a) implement Linux CI, (b) keep macOS-only with cost
  acceptance, (c) defer indefinitely with conditions for
  re-evaluation).
- This bead closes with a clear outcome.

## Testing
- The spike runs the existing fixture set on Linux; goldens
  are NOT updated (it's an investigation, not a CI change).
- Logging: per-fixture comparison output recorded as JSON-line:
  {"fixture":"X","platform":"linux","ssim_vs_macos_golden":F,
   "linf_vs_macos_golden":N,"determinism_class":"tight|loose|broken"}.

## Risks / considerations
- llvmpipe quality varies by Mesa version; pin a specific
  version to make the spike reproducible.
- Intermediate results are not actionable by themselves;
  present a clear recommendation, not raw numbers.
- This is a stretch — do NOT block tier-1 GPU work on it.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- linux
- stretch
- cost

## References
- GPU_CI (ft-ombfl.13) — the macOS-pinned baseline.
- Mesa llvmpipe documentation.
- GitHub Actions runner pricing.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cod_3","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:15.317496Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:39:36.850386Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cost","gpu-harness","linux","postswarm-2026-04-27","stretch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.16","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:15.317496Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.16","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.13","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-28T01:10:20.755590Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-ombfl.2","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness] Harness scaffold: tests/golden/gpu/ layout, comparator, fixture loader, JSON-line logging","description":"# GPU regression harness — scaffold

## Background
After the design doc (GPU_DESIGN) lands, this bead implements the
core scaffolding: the test directory tree, the golden-image
comparator, the fixture loader, and the harness binary that ties
them together. Every fixture child bead consumes this scaffold.

## Why this matters
This is THE prerequisite for all fixture work. Without a working
scaffold, fixture beads have no harness to test against. Get this
right and fixtures become pure data + a one-line registration call.

## Scope
1. Create tests/golden/gpu/ directory with a README.md describing
   layout (per the design doc's section 5).
2. Implement the fixture-loader: reads tests/golden/gpu/<name>/
   directory, returns (input-spec, golden-png, meta.json, expected.json).
3. Implement the comparator (per the design doc's section 4):
   - PNG load → RGBA8 buffer.
   - Per-pixel L∞ delta + SSIM (or whichever algorithm chosen).
   - Tolerance threshold from meta.json (per-fixture override).
   - Returns (PASS|FAIL, diff-report-as-PNG, metrics).
4. Implement the harness binary (or `cargo test --test gpu_regression`
   integration test) that:
   - Discovers all fixtures.
   - For each: loads input, renders via the chosen renderer, captures
     pixels, compares against golden.
   - Emits structured JSON-line log per fixture.
   - Exits 0 if all pass; non-zero with summary if any fail.
5. Add a CLI flag `--update-goldens` to RE-pin goldens after a
   reviewed change (with safety: requires interactive confirmation
   or env var SET_GOLDEN=1).

## Out of scope
- Renderer integration (separate bead).
- Fixture authoring (separate beads).
- CI wiring (separate bead).

## Approach
1. Read GPU_DESIGN's accepted decisions; treat them as binding.
2. Choose a Rust image lib (likely `image` crate) — standard for PNG.
3. Choose comparator: if SSIM, use `dssim` or `image_compare`. If
   per-pixel + tolerance, write inline (small).
4. Implement fixture loader as a struct + functions; unit-test
   against tests/golden/gpu/_smoketest/ (a fixture this bead also
   creates that exercises load/compare in isolation).
5. Implement harness binary as `tests/gpu_regression.rs` (Rust
   integration test) so cargo test runs it natively.
6. Wire JSON-line logging via `tracing` or direct stderr writes:
   {"phase":"discover","count":N},
   {"phase":"fixture","name":"X","status":"start"},
   {"phase":"fixture","name":"X","render_ms":N,"compare_ms":N,
    "ssim":F,"linf":F,"status":"pass"|"fail"},
   {"phase":"summary","total":N,"passed":N,"failed":N}.

## Acceptance criteria
- tests/golden/gpu/README.md exists and matches the design doc.
- Fixture loader, comparator, and harness binary all in place.
- Smoketest fixture (tests/golden/gpu/_smoketest/) passes via
  `cargo test --test gpu_regression`.
- JSON-line logging schema is documented and exercised.
- `--update-goldens` flag works and is gated.

## Testing
- Unit tests for the comparator: synthetic identical / different /
  near-identical-with-tolerance inputs.
- Integration test: smoketest fixture passes; mutated smoketest
  (single pixel changed) fails with clear diff output.
- E2E logging verification: capture JSON-line output; assert all
  expected events emitted.

## Risks / considerations
- If using SSIM, pick a deterministic implementation. Some libraries
  use SIMD that's not bit-reproducible across architectures.
- Don't tie comparator to a specific GPU output format yet — that's
  the renderer's concern. Accept RGBA8 + meta.json describing color
  space.
- The smoketest fixture should NOT depend on real font rendering;
  use a procedurally-generated image (gradient or checkerboard) so
  scaffold-correctness is decoupled from font-pinning.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness

- GPU_DESIGN (this bead's prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).
- frankenterm-gui crate.","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (smoketest vs --self-test + PNG metadata)

### Smoketest fixture vs --self-test flag (concrete distinction)
These are TWO different mechanisms with overlapping but distinct
purposes:

| Mechanism | What it tests | When it runs |
|---|---|---|
| Smoketest fixture (tests/golden/gpu/_smoketest/) | Static-image input through the full fixture-loader + comparator + diff-PNG path. NO renderer involved. | Always, as part of the fixture-set. |
| `--self-test` CLI flag | Programmatic exercise of comparator/loader without any fixture I/O. Synthetic in-memory test. | CI runs BEFORE the full fixture-set; locally on demand. |

The smoketest validates: golden file format, PNG decode, comparator
on real disk-roundtrip data. The --self-test validates: in-memory
correctness of comparator algorithms + error paths.

A failing smoketest while --self-test passes → likely PNG encoding
or path issue. A failing --self-test → comparator code regression.

### PNG metadata stripping (cross-ref to GPU_DESIGN)
The harness must encode goldens with deterministic PNG settings:
- No `tIME`, `tEXt`, `iTXt`, or `pHYs` chunks.
- Fixed filter (paeth) and compression level (6).
Verify on every commit via a CI lint that re-encodes goldens
with the canonical settings and asserts byte-equal output.

### Smoketest fixture content (concrete)
Author the smoketest as a 64x64 PNG containing:
- 8x8 checkerboard pattern in the top-left quadrant.
- Solid red triangle in the top-right quadrant.
- 10-line gradient in the bottom-half.
This exercises: large solid regions (compression), edges
(filter), gradient (per-pixel variation). Hand-author once,
commit, and never regenerate.

### Test framework decision verification
Before authoring tests/shell/swarm_tick_tests.bats etc., verify
that BATS is acceptable:
- Check if the project already has bats-core in CI.
- If yes: proceed.
- If no: open a small decision PR adding bats-core to CI deps.
- If maintainer prefers Rust integration tests: use
  std::process::Command-based pattern instead.

Document the decision in scripts/test-gpu-harness.sh header.

### Comparator dep verification
Before adding `image_compare` crate to Cargo.toml, verify:
- License (MIT/Apache OK; check via `cargo license`).
- Maintenance status (last release date, open issues).
- Transitive dep size.
If unacceptable, fall back to inline L∞ + skip SSIM (~30 LOC,
license-free).

### --self-test exit semantics
- 0: scaffold healthy.
- 2: scaffold broken (fixture-loader bug, comparator panic).
The harness CI step runs `--self-test` first; on non-zero,
fails the job IMMEDIATELY without running fixtures (saves CI
time on broken-scaffold runs).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:18.863269Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:23:24.200136Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:23:24.199974Z","close_reason":"GPU harness scaffold landed: custom gpu_regression harness, tests/golden/gpu fixture layout, fixture loader, comparator, JSON-line logs, update-goldens gate, and _smoketest fixture; verified with rch fmt/self-test/fixture run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.2","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:18.863269Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.2","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:04.061075Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.3","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-harness] Renderer integration: hook frankenterm-gui render path to offscreen-capture surface","description":"# GPU harness — renderer integration

## Background
With the harness scaffold (GPU_SCAFFOLD) in place, this bead wires
the frankenterm-gui render path to write into the harness's
offscreen-capture surface so fixtures can be rendered deterministically.

## Why this matters
Without this hook, the harness can't produce a real frame from a real
input — it can only test against pre-rendered images, which defeats
the purpose. This integration is the bridge between the test harness
and the production render code.

## Scope
1. Add a render entrypoint in frankenterm-gui (or a helper crate)
   that:
   - Takes a fixture-input-spec (terminal grid + cursor + selection
     state + viewport size + dpi).
   - Initializes the renderer in headless mode with the chosen
     offscreen surface (per design doc).
   - Renders one frame.
   - Returns the captured RGBA8 buffer + per-frame metrics
     (render_ms, fonts loaded, glyphs cached).
2. Wire this entrypoint into the harness binary so each fixture
   registration becomes:
     - load fixture input
     - render → buffer
     - compare against golden
3. Ensure the render path is deterministic:
   - Pinned font set (from tests/fixtures/fonts/).
   - Pinned color space.
   - Disable any animation/cursor-blink.
   - Disable IME composition.
4. Document the headless-mode init knobs in the new entrypoint's
   doc-comments.

## Out of scope
- Fixture authoring.
- CI runner choice (covered by ci-wiring).
- Performance budgeting (frame-time-reporter bead).

## Approach
1. Locate frankenterm-gui's existing render entry — likely in
   crates/frankenterm-gui/src/main.rs or a renderer module.
2. Identify the abstraction layer cleanest to hook (after layout,
   before window-manager swap).
3. Add `pub fn render_headless(input: &FixtureInput) ->
   anyhow::Result<HeadlessFrame>` (or similar) gated by a
   `headless-render` Cargo feature.
4. The HeadlessFrame struct contains rgba: Vec<u8>, width, height,
   dpi, render_ms, glyphs_cached.
5. Update the harness binary to call render_headless().
6. Verify against the smoketest fixture: render_headless returns
   a buffer that matches a hand-checked golden.

## Acceptance criteria
- New render_headless entry exists, doc-commented, feature-gated.
- Smoketest fixture renders the same frame across 10 invocations
  (determinism check).
- Captured frame is byte-equal across two runs on same machine.
- Across machines (developer macOS vs CI runner), frames are within
  the comparator tolerance (SSIM ≥ 0.99, L∞ within threshold).
- The headless feature flag does NOT change non-headless render
  behavior.

## Testing
- Determinism test: render the smoketest fixture 10 times, assert
  byte-equal output. Log each render_ms.
- Cross-machine determinism: render on developer + CI; assert
  comparator-pass.
- Negative test: change an input field (cursor pos); assert frame
  differs in expected region only.
- E2E logging: render path emits {"phase":"render-init"},
  {"phase":"render-frame","ms":N,"glyphs":N}, etc.

## Risks / considerations
- macOS uses Metal; CI Linux runner uses something else
  (llvmpipe/swrast). Cross-platform determinism is the hardest
  part of this work — be prepared to widen the comparator
  tolerance OR pin to one runner.
- font fallback: if the harness uses the system font fallback chain,
  test results depend on machine-installed fonts. Pin the font dir.
- IME state, mouse-hover state, focused-pane state: all must be
  fully specified by FixtureInput. No implicit globals.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD (prerequisite).
- frankenterm-gui crate.
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (CI fallback + missing-GPU handling)

### Failure mode: missing system GPU libraries
On CI runners, GPU libraries may be unavailable or partially
configured (especially on minimal Linux runners or Docker
containers). The renderer must fail FAST with a clear error
rather than hang or silently produce noise.

Init sequence with explicit checks:
1. Try GPU init (Metal on macOS / Vulkan on Linux).
2. If init fails: `Result::Err(HeadlessError::GpuInitFailed {
   platform: "...", driver: "...", reason: "..." })`.
3. Harness binary catches this error and exits with code 2
   (harness-error, not regression).
4. CI step interprets exit-2 as "infra-broken" not
   "regression-detected".

### CI runner GPU readiness probe (separate step)
Before running the harness in CI, run a readiness probe:
```yaml
- name: Probe GPU availability
  run: |
    cargo run --release --bin gpu-readiness-probe \
      --features gpu-headless 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gpu-probe.log
- name: Run harness
  if: success()
  run: scripts/test-gpu-harness.sh
```
The probe binary (a sibling of the harness) does a minimal
init + readback. If it fails, the run fails fast without
running fixtures.

### Software-fallback option (stretch)
If GPU init fails, optionally fall back to a CPU/software
renderer (e.g., Mesa softpipe). Two sub-options:
- (a) Run fixtures on the software fallback BUT mark them as
  "software-mode" in the JSON-line summary; baseline goldens
  are still hardware (so will fail). This is INFORMATIVE
  ONLY — doesn't gate.
- (b) Have a separate set of "software-mode goldens" pinned
  for the software renderer; both gates.
- (c) Refuse software fallback and require hardware GPU.

**Recommend (c)** for v1: simpler, cleaner signal. File (a) or
(b) as a follow-on if a software-only runner becomes desirable.

### Driver / API-version logging
At init, emit a JSON-line event capturing:
- GPU vendor / model (`MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice` on macOS).
- Driver version (Metal feature set, or Mesa version on Linux).
- API version.
- Available memory.
This metadata lands in every fixture's per-run log so determinism
deltas can be diagnosed without re-running.

### Rust toolchain pin (cross-ref to GPU_DESIGN)
GPU_DESIGN's notes mention pinning rust-toolchain.toml. The
renderer's headless feature must be tested AT the pinned
toolchain. Adding `cargo build --features gpu-headless` to a
toolchain-bump PR is mandatory.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.310753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:40:27.307857Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:40:27.307597Z","close_reason":"headless GUI render harness entrypoint wired","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.3","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.310753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.3","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:04.478870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.4","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Basic ASCII text fixture: simple paragraph + line wrap + scrollback","description":"# GPU fixture — basic ASCII text

## Background
First fixture beyond the smoketest. Validates that the harness can
correctly render normal terminal output: ASCII text, line wrapping,
scrollback.

## Why this matters
- Establishes the fixture-authoring pattern that subsequent fixtures
  follow.
- Catches a class of regressions (off-by-one in wrap point, baseline
  shift, line-spacing) that affect every other fixture.

## Scope
1. Author tests/golden/gpu/text-basic-paragraph/:
   - input.json: terminal grid 80×24 with a 5-line paragraph
     of mixed ASCII (no fancy unicode), one line that exceeds 80 cols
     to test wrap, an empty line, and a final line of dashes.
   - meta.json: viewport 80×24, dpi 1.0, tolerance defaults.
   - expected.json: list of glyph regions (optional sanity check).
   - golden.png: the rendered frame (generated by --update-goldens
     in a controlled environment, then committed).
2. Author tests/golden/gpu/text-scrollback/:
   - input.json: 80×24 viewport but 100 lines of content (scrollback).
   - meta.json: scroll position to bottom.
   - golden.png: shows the bottom-of-scrollback view.

## Out of scope
- Unicode / CJK / emoji (separate fixture).
- Interactive scrolling animation (this is static rendering).

## Approach
1. Write the fixture inputs by hand (small JSON files).
2. Run the harness with --update-goldens to generate golden.png in
   a dev-laptop controlled environment.
3. Manually inspect goldens for sanity (open in image viewer).
4. Commit fixtures + goldens.
5. Verify CI passes (after ci-wiring lands).

## Acceptance criteria
- Both fixtures (text-basic-paragraph, text-scrollback) committed.
- Each runs to PASS in `cargo test --test gpu_regression`.
- Each emits the standard JSON-line log events.
- Manual inspection confirms goldens are visually correct.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test: `cargo test --test gpu_regression text` runs
  only these two fixtures and passes.
- Mutation test: change cell content in input.json by one char;
  confirm fixture fails with a diff PNG that visually shows the
  changed cell.
- Logging verification: capture JSON-line output for both fixtures;
  assert one fixture event per fixture.

## Risks / considerations
- Don't include line-trailing whitespace differences — the comparator
  must already be tolerant or the fixture must avoid them.
- Pin a deterministic, available font for the basic tests (e.g.,
  the Liberation Mono shipped under tests/fixtures/fonts/).

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD, GPU_RENDERER (prerequisites).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass 2026-04-27 (more text cases)

### Additional fixtures to author
Beyond the 2 listed in the description:
3. **text-tabs/**: a paragraph with TAB characters at column 8, 16, 24
   to verify tab-stop alignment is correct. Common renderer bug
   class: tabs interpreted as spaces or as fixed-width.
4. **text-long-line-no-break/**: an 800-char unbreakable string (no
   spaces) to verify line wrap handles non-breakable correctly.
5. **text-trailing-whitespace/**: lines with trailing spaces. Many
   terminals render selection-trailing-space differently from
   no-trailing-space. Pin the chosen behavior.
6. **text-empty-cells/**: a grid with intentionally-empty cells
   between content (e.g., bash prompt + cursor + spaces + content).
   Catches "background fill" regressions.

### Rationale
text-basic-paragraph and text-scrollback alone don't cover
tab-stop, long-line wrap, or trailing-whitespace edge cases. These 4
additional fixtures take ~20 min total to author and catch concrete
regression classes that have hit other terminals.

### Total fixtures in this bead
6 (was 2). Per-fixture work is small; total still <60 min.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.762519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:45:19.792252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixture","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.4","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.762519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.4","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:05.209659Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
